summary: Excellent Charlie Miller soundboard captures a stellar ’86 show with a soaring Keep On Growing and a fiery Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance.
review: The recording is widely praised as one of the highest‑quality tape captures in the archive, delivering crisp, balanced sound despite a few wobblier moments in the second set. Musically the concert shines with standout performances of Keep On Growing, Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance, Wharf Rat and Throwing Stones, while Terrapin and Lady With A Fan show minor slips. Overall fans describe it as an unforgettable Valentine’s Day‑era Dead experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.5★, most comments are glowing about both performance and tape quality; only minor criticisms of the second set keep it from a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Charlie Miller tape is described as crystal‑clear, high‑fidelity and one of the best recordings in the archive; a few reviewers note slight wobble in the second set but no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is consistently strong; second set mixes high‑energy peaks (Lost Sailor > Saint, Wharf Rat) with a few rough spots, but overall the concert maintains momentum and excitement.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is praised as rocking and funky, though he slips on lyrics during Lady With A Fan.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for conducting the band with his waving bass and providing a steady foundation despite health rumors.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s funky keyboard playing receives specific commendation.
